text.title: Risk Management Framework
text.summary: Risk types, four-step identification-to-monitoring process, threat and opportunity response strategies, and EMV-based contingency planning.

list.item: Threat — negative risk; probability × impact = potential loss
Opportunity — positive risk; probability × impact = potential gain; use Exploit/Enhance/Share/Accept strategies
Known risk — identified and plannable; goes in the register with a response
Unknown-unknown — cannot predict; rely on resilience design, not prevention

rule.statement: Track opportunities, not just threats; use symmetric response strategies (Exploit/Enhance/Share/Accept).
rule.rationale: Risk registers that list only threats miss half the picture. Opportunities require active responses to capture; an unmanaged opportunity is a threat of lost value.

list.item: Step 1 — Identify: brainstorm from charter + WBS + lessons learned + risk categories (technical, external, organizational, project); each risk needs a quoted source from inputs
Step 2 — Analyze: qualitative P×I matrix for all; quantitative EMV = P_numeric × impact_USD for risks that will feed contingency reserves
Step 3 — Plan Responses: assign a named owner and observable triggers; select response strategy; fund Accept responses with contingency
Step 4 — Monitor: weekly 15-minute review; compare register vs current status reports; convert materialized risks to Issues and remove from register

rule.statement: Every risk must have a named owner (a person, not "the team") and explicit observable triggers.
rule.rationale: Risks without owners are ignored. Triggers convert abstract risks into actionable events — without them, the risk stays abstract until it is already a crisis.

section.title: Response Strategies

list.item: Avoid — eliminate the risk source (e.g., remove unproven technology from scope)
Transfer — shift to third party via insurance, fixed-price contract, or warranty
Mitigate — reduce probability or impact (e.g., cross-train backup, add automated tests)
Accept — acknowledge and provision contingency equal to EMV; never accept without a budget line
Exploit — ensure the opportunity materializes (e.g., assign dedicated resource)
Enhance — increase probability or impact (e.g., early market seeding)
Share — partner with another party to maximize capture
Accept — take it if it comes without special action

rule.statement: Set contingency reserve = sum(EMV) of all Accepted threats; management reserve sits on top for unknown-unknowns.
rule.rationale: Accepting a risk without provisioning a contingency budget is hoping, not managing. EMV-based reserves give the sponsor a defensible number for the risk budget line.

section.reference: Risk register table with all required fields
Deterministic P×I scorer and EMV calculator from risks.yaml
